---
title: R Exercises
coi: true
---

# R Exercises

This chapter demonstrates R exercises with cross-origin isolation enabled
(`coi: true` in the YAML header). webR requires `SharedArrayBuffer`, which
needs COOP/COEP headers. The blendtutor filter injects the
`coi-serviceworker.js` shim when COI is activated.

## Exercise 1: Add function

Write a function `add(a, b)` that returns the sum of two numbers.

::: {.blendtutor language="r"}
Write a function `add(a, b)` that returns the sum.

```r
add <- function(a, b) { ___ }
```

```{.r .checks}
stopifnot(add(1, 2) == 3)
```

```{.r .solution}
a + b
```

::: {.hints}
Use the `<-` operator to assign the function body.
:::
:::

## Exercise 2: Greeting function

Write a function `greet(name)` that returns a greeting string.

::: {.blendtutor language="r"}
Write a function `greet(name)` that returns `"hello, <name>"`.

```r
greet <- function(name) { ___ }
```

```{.r .checks}
stopifnot(greet("world") == "hello, world")
```

```{.r .solution}
paste("hello,", name)
```
:::
